title: Lithocarpus pulcher
uses: Lithocarpus pulcher is a plant species native to China, and its bark, leaves, and roots have been traditionally used in Chinese medicine for various purposes. The plant is said to possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may be beneficial in treating certain health conditions. In traditional Chinese medicine, Lithocarpus pulcher is used to treat fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory diseases. However, more research is needed to confirm the plant's medicinal properties and potential uses. The plant's exact medicinal uses and potential benefits should be further studied and verified.
taxonomy: Lithocarpus pulcher is a species of plant in the family Fagaceae. The genus Lithocarpus is part of the order Fagales, which includes other oak and beech trees. Lithocarpus pulcher is further classified as a species of evergreen tree. This classification is based on its growth habit, leaf structure, and other morphological characteristics. The combination of these features places Lithocarpus pulcher within the broader botanical classification of Fagaceae.

intro: Lithocarpus pulcher, also known as the beautiful stone oak or Taiwan stone oak, is a species of evergreen tree in the beech family, Fagaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and digestive issues, due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. Bioactive constituents of Lithocarpus pulcher include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal effects. In traditional Chinese medicine, the plant is often used to make herbal preparations such as decoctions and infusions, as well as being dried and used as a powder or extract in capsules and tablets.
